steps.title: Spot the interrupter
steps.content: The relative clause is "that... funded major infrastructure projects." Between the subject "that" and the verb "funded," the writer inserts the prepositional phrase "through trade and centralized taxation." A comma already opens this interrupter before "through," so a matching comma must close it.

steps.title: Apply the paired-punctuation rule
steps.content: Parenthetical interrupters require matching punctuation on both sides — comma/comma or dash/dash, not mixed. The opener is a comma, so the closer must also be a comma.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'taxation:': a colon cannot close a comma-opened interrupter, and there is also a stray space before it. Wrong mark. ✗
  • Choice B — 'taxation,': a closing comma matches the opening comma after "system," properly bracketing the interrupter. ✓
  • Choice C — 'taxation —': an em dash cannot pair with an opening comma; punctuation must match. ✗
  • Choice D — 'taxation': no punctuation leaves the interrupter unclosed, fusing it with the verb "funded." ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
